meta.title: anti-

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Every word and meaning transcribed faithfully; the teaching layer (mentor_note, discovery) is authored for CAT OS.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Word DNA milestone); WORD_DNA_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: Deliberately follows ante- (wd-0005) so the predict step can name the exact confusion between the two prefixes while it is still fresh, rather than treat them as unrelated. Owner review pending.

unit.label: anti-
unit.mentor_note: Anti and ante sound almost the same and mean almost opposite things. That single letter is worth slowing down for.

members.meaning: a remedy against a poison or disease
a dislike or aversion toward something
a substance that stimulates the production of antibodies against it

discovery.notice_prompt: What do these words share, and how is it different from ante- (before)?
discovery.understand_note: Anti- means against. An antidote works against a poison. Antiseptic works against infection. Antipathy is a feeling held against someone. It shares almost every letter with ante- (before), and none of the meaning.

applies.prompt: Knowing anti- means against, what do you think an antigen does?

options.text: It triggers the body's defenses to act against it
It removes the body's need to defend itself
It existed in the body before any illness arrived
